title_guess: Wales: Elan Valley, Rhayader & St David's

pages.text:
Sunday

We visited Venetia & David in their new home "The New House" for coffee on our way home. Very smart bath rooms. Lovely home.
Soon after leaving the van mil o metre passed from 99,000 to 100,000. (Just under 4,000 per year.) On our way home to watch Boring Murray in Wimbledon Finals.
~~Friday~~ Monday August 12th

Thomas & family in France. Peter & family we've just spent a day with in new house in Tufnell Green. John has started at Bristol one week into ICU job. Charles living with future mo-in-law in Witney until Oxford ho- ready.
12.30 Simon & Eve, Tindse leave for farm. Jam on motorway, lunch, arrive farm at 3. Simon & Eve help bind hop stems around sticks & pull up weeds. Discuss small hop-drying frame with Andy - Eve suggest Queen ocluders. Watch bulldozers changing bank from J to V.

Joseph has won 5 rosettes at a gymkhana yesterday. A big break-time in his confidence. We play draughts. Or gorgeous weather Simon & I join adults at wood-work place for pyjya & chat. 2 firemen - Mr & Mrs.
Tuesday Aug 13th
We come to countryside for p.g. At 7.15 lorries arrive. By 7.45 there is one bull dozer or 2 ? 2 dumper trucks, 3 lorries delivering gravel outside (above Will + Kenny's caravan) & 3 grandchildren having break fast in our van. Simon helps William on farm. Freya takes children & me to Barnyard, shopping & Library. Back at farm physio arrives for Joseph in pool. Eve swims. lunch. Then we leave.
3.45. we reach Polwigo Campsite Llanwrthwl near Rhayader & Elan valley. We go on a lovely walk, small village.
Then read; sparkling wine, supper. Gorgeous sunshine. Cambrian Mtns.

__Wednesday__ Another glorious day. We set off about 9 to walk over the hills to see the reservoirs in the Elan Valley. Built in 1890's used 50,000 men (not all at once). Over the top Victorian arch*. We had lunch (I had a good veggie burger) at the cafe at the centre + forgot to look at the museum. As we walked round part of a lake we saw an old, broken dam that Barnes Wallace had tested to see how close bombs had to be to the Mohne dam (touching) It happened to be [illegible] 1/5 size of German dam. Ruined in 1942. It had been built to provide water for Elan dam builder (1892). We walked back to the van along pretty roads - easier because less up + down, easier in sandals + Tinder didn't constantly lunge after sheep. James Watt had a house near the campsite.
  At 2.30? we set off for St. Davids. We've reached the campsite. Oh dear rain + more rain. __Porth Clais **__

__Thursday__
Rather a severe woman took our money in the morning + complained twice that

Tinder wasn't on the lead when he bounced out of the van to greet her. Customer service not high in priorities. Simon had to argue to get the payment right as shown on website. But the loos are clean + good shower for 50p.
  We set off along the coastal path - cold damp dreary. But within an hour we cd. take our coats off. The way passed an old estuary with lime stone kilns in use for 400 years until 1900. The port may have been used by the Romans to reach St. David's. The coastal path was quite dangerous. Sheer drops. Gorgeous heather + yellow flowers. Black berries, blue sea. Beautiful. We walked down to a beach with purple stones. On to St. David's. Bought bread + chocolate. Lots of people. We had lunch. I had smoked haddock + mozzarella fish cakes. Ice cream. It rained - we just didn't need umbrellas in the restaurant - while we ate ice cream.

Back at the van we had a [illegible] relaxing after noon & walked again by the estuary. We walked 10 miles yesterday 7 today.
Friday

Rain in the night. Van buffeted by wind 24 mph with 35 mph gusts.
 We drove to St. David's carpark [strikethrough: campsite] & walked to Information building – rendez-vous for start of pilgrimage walk) to St. Non's Chapel at 10.30., only to find that the website had not been updated – the walk had start[e]d at 10. It was a ghastly morning with horizontal scuds of rain, so a relief really. We wandered round the town & admired the beautiful ceiling & walls of the cathedral. We had lunch there – quiche & roasted veg. The information centre also had an interesting exhibition on Constable. His early visits to Wales were to Porta Clais ("meeting of the waters".) We came back to the campsite & walked to see St. Non's Chapel (which we wd have walked to this morning) along the coastal path which again had sheer drops in parts. The little harbour was completely

empty of water because the tide was out St. Non gave birth to St. David just there. A holy well, enormous stones in ruined chapel. St. Non reputed to be neice of Arthur? We saw a dalmation with panniers, carrying his own food + drink.

captions: Salisbury Cathedral from the meadows 1831 Constable painted after his wife died. Great friendship with John Fisher-house where rainbow ends.
pages.text:
Salisbury Cathedral from the meadows 1831 Constable painted after his wife died. Great friendship with John Fisher-house where rainbow ends.

s out. Wind gusts up to 40 mph now.               5 saints
Saturday              Dolaucothi          Pumpsaint stone
lots of rain. [?Wnts] We walked along the cliffs + saw enormous waves crashing + cresting the cliffs. We went on [illegible] to a Gold Mine stopping at Morrisons on the way. We

both enjoyed the gold mine. We had lunch in the carpark. The engine shed showed old air compressors & electric start-up machines & a gantry. The Romans did open cast mining & collected water in huge pools (size of swimming pools) released it in one go & water scoured the hill side. They built 12 day fires against the limestone & cold water, cracks, pickaxes. Slaves worked 3 water wheel tread mills to pump water out of mines. Then 1890, Then 1930s, but poison in cold, purified in Hamburg before WWII or Canada, but eventually flooded. Reopend by the university students. Carat measurement from ~~cart~~ carob seeds. Johne - VC in India Mutiny but shot by his butler.
     We drove on to see a bridge railway via duct. It was under

scaffolding. We had to come back to Clanwrthol campsite because Simon ha[d] left coffee pot.
     Rain off + on all day. Dog patient.
Sunday
Gloomy morning. Rain. We drove over to the Elan Valley again + read in the van until the rain stopped at 12. Then we had a super bike ride. The rain only started again just as we got back!
We cycled from a bridge near a church along a cycle track that had been a railway for building the dams. We reached Pen y Garreg Dam which holds 1300 mill gall - enough to give B'ham water for 15 days.
     We had [lunch] tea in a tearoom in a house on top of a hillock, the road in a cut-away next to it. A very nice ride.
Returning at 2.30, we had tea + a slow drive back to B'ham.
